practicals.title: Laboratory Activities (Prescribed Books)
practicals.details: Perform lab activities from 'Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX' and 'Mathematics exemplar problems'. Includes constructing bisectors of line segments/angles, triangles, polygons satisfying constraints, circumcircle/circumcentre construction, and verifying geometric properties.

activities.title: Construct the Square Root Spiral
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral to visualize irrational numbers.

activities.title: Visualise Algebraic Identities using Geometrical Models
activities.details: Use algebra tiles or geometric models to visualise identities and factorisation.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to understand recursive rules and computational thinking.

activities.title: Floor Plan Representation on Grid
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates to specify locations.

activities.title: Geometric Constructions with Sulbasutras
activities.details: Construct a square with a given side as described in Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Paper Manipulation for 4-gons
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Analysis
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, such as Dharmachakra and Ashoka Chakra.

activities.title: Squaring a Rectangle Proof
activities.details: Derive the proof for squaring a rectangle from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Data Collection and Graphical Representation
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data to answer a statistical investigative question using stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Random Experiment Simulation
activities.details: Use computational thinking strategies like simulation and pattern recognition to model random experiments.

projects.title: Historical Approximations of Pi Research Project
projects.details: Research historical approximations to pi from Archimedes, Aryabhata, and Zu Chongzhi.

projects.title: Real-life Applications of Geometry Project
projects.details: Investigate how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology.

practicals.title: Laboratory Activities (Prescribed Books)
practicals.details: Perform lab activities from 'Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX' and 'Mathematics exemplar problems'. Includes constructing bisectors of line segments/angles, triangles, polygons satisfying constraints, circumcircle/circumcentre construction, and verifying geometric properties.

activities.title: Construct the Square Root Spiral
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral to visualize irrational numbers.

activities.title: Visualise Algebraic Identities using Geometrical Models
activities.details: Use algebra tiles or geometric models to visualise identities and factorisation.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to understand recursive rules and computational thinking.

activities.title: Floor Plan Representation on Grid
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates to specify locations.

activities.title: Geometric Constructions with Sulbasutras
activities.details: Construct a square with a given side as described in Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Paper Manipulation for 4-gons
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Analysis
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, such as Dharmachakra and Ashoka Chakra.

activities.title: Squaring a Rectangle Proof
activities.details: Derive the proof for squaring a rectangle from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Data Collection and Graphical Representation
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data to answer a statistical investigative question using stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Random Experiment Simulation
activities.details: Use computational thinking strategies like simulation and pattern recognition to model random experiments.

projects.title: Historical Approximations of Pi Research Project
projects.details: Research historical approximations to pi from Archimedes, Aryabhata, and Zu Chongzhi.

projects.title: Real-life Applications of Geometry Project
projects.details: Investigate how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology.

marks_distribution: Unit VI: Statistics and Probability - 10 Marks

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding or solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge.
Examine information, present and defend opinions, or compile information in new patterns.

competency_groups.title: Understands numbers and number sets
competency_groups.description: Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.
competencies: C-1.1: Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.

competency_groups.title: Builds deductive and inductive logic for number proofs
competencies: C-2.1: Understanding of powers (radical powers) and exponents.

competency_groups.title: Discovers algebraic identities and models real-life situations with equations
competency_groups.description: States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, division algorithm; models contextualized problems using equations.
competencies: C-3.1: States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, and division algorithm.

C-3.2: Models and solves contextualised problems using equations (for example, simultaneous linear equations in two variables or single polynomial equations), and draws conclusions about a situation being modelled.

competency_groups.title: Analyses characteristics of 2D geometric shapes and develops mathematical arguments
competency_groups.description: Describes relationships including congruence; proves theorems using Euclid's axioms for triangles, quadrilaterals, circles.
competencies: C-4.1: Describes relationships including congruence of two-dimensional geometric shapes (such as lines, angles, triangles) to make and test conjectures and solve problems.
C-4.2: Proves theorems using Euclid's axioms and postulates for triangles and quadrilaterals, and applies them to solve geometric problems.

competency_groups.title: Derives and uses formulae for areas, surface area, and volumes
competency_groups.description: Visualises Heron's formula; visualises mathematical thinking to discover formulae for solid objects.
competencies: C-5.1: Visualises, represents, and calculates the area of a triangle using Heron’s formula and its generalisation to cyclic quadrilaterals given by Brahmagupta’s formula.

competency_groups.title: Analyses data using statistical concepts and probability
competency_groups.description: Applies measures of central tendencies; applies concepts from probability to solve problems.
competencies: C-6.1: Applies measures of central tendencies, such as mean, median, and mode.

competency_groups.title: Perceives the axiomatic structure of Mathematics
competency_groups.description: Proves mathematical statements; visualises geometric proofs for algebraic identities.
competencies: C-7.1: Proves mathematical statements and carries out geometric constructions using stated assumptions, axioms, postulates, definitions, and mathematics vocabulary.

competency_groups.title: Builds skills in visualisation, optimisation, representation, and modelling
competency_groups.description: Models daily-life phenomena; uses 2D representations of 3D objects.
competencies: C-8.1: Models daily-life phenomena and uses representations, such as graphs, tables, and equations to draw conclusions.

competency_groups.title: Develops computational thinking
competency_groups.description: Decomposes problems; describes sequences of instructions.
competencies: C-9.1: Decomposes a problem into sub-problems.

competency_groups.title: Knows and appreciates contributions of mathematicians from India and around the world
competency_groups.description: Recognises important contributions made by mathematicians.
competencies: C-10.1: Recognises the important contributions made by mathematicians (Indian and others) in the field of Mathematics.

competency_groups.title: Explores connections of Mathematics with other subjects
competency_groups.description: Applies mathematical knowledge and tools to analyse problems in multiple subjects.
competencies: C-11.1: Applies mathematical knowledge and tools to analyse problems or situations in multiple subjects across Science, Social Science, Visual Arts, Music, Vocational Education, and Sports.

practicals.title: Laboratory Activities (Prescribed Books)
practicals.details: Perform lab activities from 'Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX' and 'Mathematics exemplar problems'. Includes constructing bisectors of line segments/angles, triangles, polygons satisfying constraints, circumcircle/circumcentre construction, and verifying geometric properties.

activities.title: Construct the Square Root Spiral
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral to visualize irrational numbers.

activities.title: Visualise Algebraic Identities using Geometrical Models
activities.details: Use algebra tiles or geometric models to visualise identities and factorisation.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to understand recursive rules and computational thinking.

activities.title: Floor Plan Representation on Grid
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates to specify locations.

activities.title: Geometric Constructions with Sulbasutras
activities.details: Construct a square with a given side as described in Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Paper Manipulation for 4-gons
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Analysis
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, such as Dharmachakra and Ashoka Chakra.

activities.title: Squaring a Rectangle Proof
activities.details: Derive the proof for squaring a rectangle from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Data Collection and Graphical Representation
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data to answer a statistical investigative question using stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Random Experiment Simulation
activities.details: Use computational thinking strategies like simulation and pattern recognition to model random experiments.

projects.title: Historical Approximations of Pi Research Project
projects.details: Research historical approximations to pi from Archimedes, Aryabhata, and Zu Chongzhi.

projects.title: Real-life Applications of Geometry Project
projects.details: Investigate how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology.
